No Poverty.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course builds professional knowledge (Target 4.4) and includes a core competency to 'identify ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability issues,' directly supporting education for sustainable development (Target 4.7).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course covers 'Cost Management,' 'Inventory Management,' and 'Performance Measurement,' which enhance economic productivity (Target 8.2) and resource efficiency (Target 8.4), fostering sustainable economic growth.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: 'Capital Budgeting' and 'Cost Management' are key for strategic investments in upgrading industrial infrastructure for sustainability and resource efficiency (Target 9.4), supported by sustainability issues.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course's core competency to 'identify social responsibility issues' and topics like 'Multinational Considerations' offer a very weak, inferred link to policies addressing inequalities (Target 10.4).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: 'Inventory Management' and 'Just-in-Time' promote efficient resource use and waste reduction (Targets 12.2, 12.5). The 'Balanced Scorecard' can integrate sustainability reporting (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: 'Capital Budgeting' provides tools for long-term investments that can integrate climate change measures into corporate planning (Target 13.2), supported by the 'sustainability issues' competency.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to marine ecosystems.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence in the course syllabus directly links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: 'Management Control Systems' and 'Performance Measurement' are vital for effective, accountable institutions (Target 16.6). The course's emphasis on 'ethics' supports reducing corruption (Target 16.5).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Topics like 'Transfer Pricing' and 'Multinational Considerations' relate to international financial flows and corporate policy, offering a weak, indirect link to global macroeconomic stability and policy coherence (Targets 17.13, 17.14).
